name: Exchange Bank
description_short: Community-focused bank serving Sonoma County and North Bay since 1890 with personal banking, business services, and investment management.
description_long:
Exchange Bank has operated as a community bank in Sonoma County for 135 years, founded in 1890 with deep roots in North Bay economic development. The bank traces its heritage to co-founder Frank Doyle, whose vision extended to supporting major infrastructure projects like the Golden Gate Bridge. Today, Exchange Bank operates 18 physical locations throughout Sonoma County and the North Bay, providing traditional banking services with a commitment to local reinvestment.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking products including checking and savings accounts with rewards options, online and mobile banking platforms, and access to 55,000 Allpoint ATMs worldwide. Business banking services include business accounts and lending to support small and medium enterprises. Exchange Bank also provides trust and investment management services, including retirement planning and wealth management, though these products are not FDIC-insured and carry investment risk.

Exchange Bank distinguishes itself through its explicit community commitment: 100% of charitable giving remains in the local community, and the bank has funded $105 million in Doyle Scholarships for education. This local-first approach contrasts with national and regional chain banks, positioning the bank as a community-reinvestment institution rather than a profit-extraction entity. The bank is FDIC-insured and regulated under NMLS ID 643948.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—Exchange Bank operates exclusively in Sonoma County and the North Bay region, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this area. The website provides limited details on specific account features, interest rates, fees, or lending terms, requiring customers to contact the bank directly or visit branches for detailed product information. For consumers in the service area seeking a community-focused banking alternative with local accountability, Exchange Bank presents a viable option.
services: Personal checking and savings accounts with rewards options
Online banking platform for account management
Mobile banking application with account access
ATM access through Allpoint network (55,000 locations worldwide)
Branch banking at 18 locations in Sonoma County and North Bay
Account opening and enrollment in online banking
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
100% of charitable donations stay in local Sonoma County community
$105 million in Doyle Scholarships funded for local education
135 years of continuous community service and local reinvestment track record
18 physical branch locations throughout Sonoma County and North Bay
Access to 55,000 Allpoint ATMs worldwide plus local branches
Online and mobile banking available for convenient account management
Trust and investment management services for retirement and wealth planning
cons: Limited to Sonoma County and North Bay geographic service area only
Website provides minimal detail on specific account features, rates, or fees
Trust and investment products not FDIC-insured and subject to market risk
Requires direct contact or branch visit for detailed product information and pricing
No indication of competitive interest rates or fee structures compared to alternatives
best_for: Sonoma County and North Bay residents seeking community-focused banking with local reinvestment
Small business owners in the region needing business banking and local relationship lending
Consumers prioritizing community charitable impact and local economic support over national banks
Individuals wanting trust and investment management with fiduciary relationships in their community
diagnosis: Exchange Bank is best for Sonoma County and North Bay residents who value community banking and local reinvestment over national chain alternatives, and who can access one of their 18 branch locations. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank does not serve customers outside Sonoma County and the North Bay region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
